steps.title: Identify the decisive comparison
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts kanamycin alters metal uptake. Because the graph compares two conditions—plants with and without kanamycin—the supporting evidence must show a difference in metal content between them.

steps.title: Match the data pattern
steps.content:
From the graph, kanamycin-exposed plants contain lower levels of both iron (≈220 ppm vs. ≈620 ppm in controls) and zinc (≈300 ppm vs. ≈390 ppm in controls), demonstrating that kanamycin reduced metal uptake.

Choices A and C misread the graph values; choice B is factually true but shows no difference between conditions and thus doesn't support altered uptake.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
